arabic_source.entry:
٥

مَدَنِيَّة إِلَّا آيَة مِنْهَا نزلت بِعَرَفَة وَهِي قَوْله تَعَالَى (﴿الْيَوْم أكملت لكم دينكُمْ﴾) إِلَى قَوْله تَعَالَى (﴿ورضيت لكم الْإِسْلَام دينا﴾) حَدثنَا عبد الرَّحْمَن بن خَالِد قَالَ أَنا أَحْمد بن جَعْفَر قَالَ أَنا عبد الله بن أَحْمد قَالَ أَنا أبي قَالَ أَنا جَعْفَر بن عون قَالَ أَنا أَبُو عُمَيْس عَن قيس بن مُسلم عَن طَارق بن شهَاب قَالَ قَالَ عمر نزلت هَذِه الْآيَة (﴿الْيَوْم أكملت لكم دينكُمْ﴾) على رَسُول الله عَشِيَّة عَرَفَة فِي يَوْم جُمُعَة
ونظيرتها فِي الْمدنِي الأول والشامي هود وَلَا نَظِير لَهَا فِي غَيرهمَا وكلمها أَلفَانِ وثمان مئة وَأَرْبع كَلِمَات
وحروفها أحد عشر ألفا وَسبع مئة وَثَلَاثَة وَثَلَاثُونَ حرفا
وَهِي مئة وَعِشْرُونَ آيَة فِي الْكُوفِي وَعِشْرُونَ وآيتان فِي الْمَدَنِيين والمكي والشامي وَعِشْرُونَ وَثَلَاث فِي البصرى
اختلافها ثَلَاث آيَات (﴿أَوْفوا بِالْعُقُودِ﴾ وَيَعْفُو عَن كثير) لم يعدهما الْكُوفِي وعدهما الْبَاقُونَ (﴿فَإِنَّكُم غالبون﴾) عدهَا الْبَصْرِيّ وَلم يعدها الْبَاقُونَ
وفيهَا مِمَّا يشبه الفواصل وَلَيْسَ معدودا بِإِجْمَاع خَمْسَة مَوَاضِع
(﴿اثْنَي عشر نَقِيبًا﴾ قوما جبارين) (﴿سماعون لقوم آخَرين﴾ أَفَحكم الْجَاهِلِيَّة يَبْغُونَ) (﴿من الَّذين اسْتحق عَلَيْهِم الأوليان﴾) على قِرَاءَة من قَرَأَ بِالْجمعِ

english_translation.entry_translation:
Sūrat al-Māʾida.

Medinan, except for one verse from it that was sent down at ʿArafa: namely His saying, exalted is He, “al-yawma akmaltu lakum dīnakum” up to His saying, “wa-raḍītu lakumu l-islāma dīnan.” ʿAbd al-Raḥmān b. Khālid related to us; he said: Aḥmad b. Jaʿfar related to us; he said: ʿAbd Allāh b. Aḥmad related to us; he said: my father related to us; he said: Jaʿfar b. ʿAwn related to us; he said: Abū ʿUmays related to us, from Qays b. Muslim, from Ṭāriq b. Shihāb, who said: ʿUmar said, “This verse — ‘al-yawma akmaltu lakum dīnakum’ — was revealed to the Messenger of God on the evening of ʿArafa, on a Friday.”

Its counterpart (naẓīrah) in the Madani-first and Shami counts is Sūrat Hūd; it has no counterpart in the others. Its words are two thousand eight hundred and four, and its letters are eleven thousand seven hundred and thirty-three.

It is one hundred and twenty verses in the Kufi count; one hundred and twenty-two in the two Madani counts, the Makki, and the Shami; and one hundred and twenty-three in the Basri.

Its points of disagreement are three verse-endings: “awfū bi-l-ʿuqūd” and “wa-yaʿfū ʿan kathīr” are not counted by the Kufi but are counted by the rest; “fa-innakum ghālibūn” is counted by the Basri and is not counted by the rest.

In it there are, by consensus, five places that resemble verse-endings without being counted: “ithnay ʿashara naqīban”; “qawman jabbārīn”; “sammāʿūna li-qawmin ākharīn”; “a-fa-ḥukma l-jāhiliyyati yabghūn”; and “min alladhīna stuḥiqqa ʿalayhimu l-awlayāni,” according to the reading of the one who reads it in the plural.
english_translation.ruus_translation_note: The running رؤوس الآي are normalized here as a complete ordered sequence on the later-Madani baseline used elsewhere in the project. The local witness is defective at the top of the block: it preserves the unnumbered extra head “بالعقود”, then resumes only at “مبين / ١٥ مستقيم ...” and runs to a terminal blank “١٢٠”. In practice, that means the witness has lost the early common heads through “يصنعون”, omitted the extra disputed head “ويعفو عن كثير”, and dropped the expected display numeral before “مبين”. The reconstructed ordered list below restores ordinals 2–16 explicitly and keeps the surviving later segment as transmitted.

structured_data.source_alignment_note_en: The entry text was normalized from the uploaded witness. The 1994 printed PDF witness confirms the entry wording and the same defective opening in the ruʾūs block: it shows “بالعقود / مبين / ١٥ مستقيم ...” and ends with a blank “١٢٠”. The normalized ruʾūs sequence therefore preserves the transmitted later segment, restores the missing early interval explicitly, and records the defect instead of silently treating the witness as complete.

classification_claims.exception_phrase_ar: الْيَوْم أكملت لكم دينكُمْ ... ورضيت لكم الْإِسْلَام دينا

letter_count.raw_ar: أحد عشر ألفا وَسبع مئة وَثَلَاثَة وَثَلَاثُونَ حرفا

supporting_transmission_reports.raw_ar: حَدثنَا عبد الرَّحْمَن بن خَالِد قَالَ أَنا أَحْمد بن جَعْفَر قَالَ أَنا عبد الله بن أَحْمد قَالَ أَنا أبي قَالَ أَنا جَعْفَر بن عون قَالَ أَنا أَبُو عُمَيْس عَن قيس بن مُسلم عَن طَارق بن شهَاب قَالَ قَالَ عمر نزلت هَذِه الْآيَة (﴿الْيَوْم أكملت لكم دينكُمْ﴾) على رَسُول الله عَشِيَّة عَرَفَة فِي يَوْم جُمُعَة
supporting_transmission_reports.summary_en: ʿUmar reported that “al-yawma akmaltu lakum dīnakum” was revealed on the evening of ʿArafa, on a Friday.
supporting_transmission_reports.chain_ar: عبد الرَّحْمَن بن خَالِد ← أَحْمد بن جَعْفَر ← عبد الله بن أَحْمد ← أبي ← جَعْفَر بن عون ← أَبُو عُمَيْس ← قيس بن مُسلم ← طَارق بن شهَاب ← عمر

structured_data.ruus_al_ayat_numbering_note_en: The running رؤوس الآي are normalized here as a complete ordered sequence on the later-Madani baseline used elsewhere in the project. The local witness is defective at the top of the block: it preserves the unnumbered extra head “بالعقود”, then resumes only at “مبين / ١٥ مستقيم ...” and runs to a terminal blank “١٢٠”. In practice, that means the witness has lost the early common heads through “يصنعون”, omitted the extra disputed head “ويعفو عن كثير”, and dropped the expected display numeral before “مبين”. The reconstructed ordered list below restores ordinals 2–16 explicitly and keeps the surviving later segment as transmitted.

ruus_al_ayat.extraction_note_en: Survives at the top of the local ruʾūs witness as an unnumbered extra head; the main entry also marks it as counted by all except the Kufi.

The extra head “ويعفو عن كثير” is stated explicitly in the source entry as counted by all except the Kufi, but it is absent from the surviving ruʾūs page. It is restored here in its expected position before “مبين”.
Survives as an unnumbered “مبين” at the top of the local ruʾūs page; its expected preceding display numeral is absent because the witness resumes mid-block after an early loss.
This and the following head are joined across one OCR line in the checked parallel PDF witness; the local text witness preserves them on separate lines.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: The five pseudo-fawasil in سورة المائدة are serialized inside one running sentence rather than as a neatly itemized list.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The five-item extraction is reliable, but it requires editorial splitting of the serialized phrasing.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: The local ruʾūs witness preserves the opening extra head “بالعقود”, then resumes only at “مبين / ١٥ مستقيم ...”. The whole early block through “يصنعون” is absent.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: Ordinals 2–15 must be reconstructed explicitly rather than treated as present in the witness.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: The disputed non-Kufi head “ويعفو عن كثير”, stated in the entry itself, is absent from the surviving ruʾūs page between “يصنعون” and “مبين”.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The normalized later-Madani sequence must restore this head explicitly at ordinal 16.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: At the point where the witness resumes, “مبين” survives without its expected display numeral, after which the page continues “١٥ مستقيم، ١٦ قدير ...”.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The surviving witness must be treated as resuming mid-display layer rather than as a clean start of sequence.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: The checked parallel PDF witness compresses several adjacent lines across page boundaries, including “٤٤ الظالمون٤٥ للمتقين”, “٧٧ يعتدون٧٨ يفعلون”, and “١١٠ مسلمون١١١ مؤمنين”.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The ordered list remains recoverable, but those boundary joins should not be re-imported as single heads.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: After the final surviving head “قدير”, the witness carries a standalone blank “١٢٠” line.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: This terminal line functions as a display total and must not be treated as an additional head.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: The surviving display numerals are not a second normalized ordinal layer. They reflect the printed witness’s own running display scheme, while extra disputed heads appear without numerals.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The dataset preserves the ordered later-Madani sequence 1–122 rather than reproducing the defective witness numbering as a primary key.
